raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
For February 2026, total actuals of £10,286 came in £379 (3.6%) under the £10,665 budget. Beneath this modest overall underspend, three line items carried material individual variances that broadly offset one another across the period.
Advertising & Marketing overspent by £667 (+66.7%) due to a deliberately accelerated spring campaign, pulled forward from March to February with MD approval. Repairs & Maintenance overspent by £786 (+786.3%) owing to an emergency HVAC failure in the server room on 14 February, with the £886 invoice approved by the CFO on the same day. Both overruns are supported by formal approvals and are considered non-recurring in nature. Subscriptions recorded a -£1,200 (-100.0%) underspend against budget with no context identified across any available source; this warrants prompt investigation to determine whether the spend has been cancelled, reclassified, or is delayed into a future period.
The significant-line net variance across these three items is +£253 on a combined budget of £2,300, with the remaining lines collectively within acceptable tolerance. No further structural concerns are identified at this stage beyond the Subscriptions gap.

LINE COMMENTARY

Advertising & Marketing | +£667 (+66.7%) | Budget: £1,000 | Actual: £1,667
The Q2 spring campaign was pulled forward from March to February at the agency's recommendation, citing a weather-sensitive launch window. The campaign team confirmed a February commitment of approximately £1,600 against the original £1,000 plan on 3 February, and MD approval for the acceleration was formally issued on 1 February. The campaign kicked off on 5 February per calendar records. The overspend is a timing variance rather than incremental budget consumption — March is likely to show a corresponding underspend in this line — though confirmation of the reforecast note referenced in the approval email is recommended.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-23T12:15:00Z - slack-advertising_&_marketing-broad - marketing-slack-2026-02-03: Campaign team — moving the Q2 spring campaign launch to this month. Client approval came in early, let's capitalise. Total Feb commitment will be ~£1,600 vs the £1,000 March plan. Approvals from MD signed off.
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:15:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-broad - gmail-2026-02-01-campaign-approval: MD approval for Q2 campaign acceleration. Spring campaign moved from March to February at agency recommendation — weather-sensitive launch window. Budget reforecast note attached.
- Calendar - 2024-11-23T22:15:00Z - calendar-advertising_&_marketing-broad - cal-2026-02-01: Marketing launch meeting — Spring campaign kick-off moved to 5 February. Agency briefed on 1 February per Slack decision.

Repairs Maintenance | +£786 (+786.3%) | Budget: £100 | Actual: £886
The HVAC unit in the server room failed overnight on 13–14 February, necessitating an emergency engineer callout. FastAir Engineering attended on 14 February (site access blocked 08:00–18:00) and raised an invoice of £886.30, inclusive of parts and an emergency callout rate. The CFO approved the spend on the same day. This is an unplanned, non-recurring event with no further spend indicated in the evidence; however, any follow-on maintenance or warranty work arising from the failure should be monitored in March.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-repairs_maintenance-broad - ops-slack-2026-02-14: URGENT — HVAC unit in server room failed overnight. Building manager called emergency engineer. Quote came in at £886. CFO approved. Works completed today.
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-broad - gmail-2026-02-14-hvac-invoice: Invoice from FastAir Engineering — emergency HVAC repair 14 Feb 2026. £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate. CFO approved same day.
- Calendar - 2024-11-19T22:15:00Z - calendar-repairs_maintenance-broad - cal-2026-02-14: Emergency maintenance — HVAC server room. Blocked out 08:00–18:00 BST for engineer access.

Subscriptions | -£1,200 (-100.0%) | Budget: £1,200 | Actual: £0
Actuals recorded nil spend against a £1,200 budget — a full-period underspend. No context was identified across Slack, Gmail (broad and narrow searches), or any other available source. It is not possible to determine from available evidence whether this reflects a cancellation, a billing cycle shift, a reclassification to another line, or a delayed invoice. No context found — recommend review with the relevant budget owner to confirm the status of the underlying subscriptions and whether any catch-up charge is expected in a subsequent period.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following line items recorded variances below the significance threshold and have not been investigated individually. They are noted here for completeness.
- Sales | +£506 (+8.4%)
- Purchases | -£500 (-100.0%)
- Audit & Accountancy fees | -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees | -£15 (-50.0%)
- Cleaning | -£1 (-0.8%)
- Entertainment - 0% | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 100% business | +£13 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses | -£254 (-211.7%)
- Light Power Heating | +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses | -£71 (-54.6%)
- Postage Freight Courier | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing Stationery | -£20 (-100.0%)
- Rent | -£46 (-4.4%)
- Telephone Internet | -£6 (-13.3%)
- Travel National | -£150 (-100.0%)
